[Unit].description: Generic unit configuration. Defines dependencies, ordering, conditions. Present in all unit types.

[Service].description: Service-specific configuration: startup type, exec commands, restart policy, resource limits, security sandbox.

[Install].description: Installation info for systemctl enable/disable. Defines which target pulls this unit.

[Timer].description: Timer configuration. Calendar or monotonic schedules. Replaces cron with more features.

[Socket].description: Socket activation. Systemd listens, starts service on connection. Enables zero-downtime restarts.

[Path].description: Path-based activation. Watches filesystem paths and starts a unit when conditions are met.

[Mount].description: Mount point configuration. Equivalent to fstab entries but managed by systemd.

[Slice].description: Resource control configuration for a group of units using cgroup hierarchy.

Description.description: Human-readable description of the unit. Shown in status output and logs.

Documentation.description: URIs pointing to documentation. Supports http://, https://, file:, info:, man: schemes.

After.description: Ordering dependency — start this unit after the listed units. Does NOT create a requirement, only ordering.

Before.description: Start this unit before the listed units.

Requires.description: Hard requirement. If any required unit fails to start, this unit is also not started. Use with After= for ordering.

Wants.description: Soft requirement. Like Requires but this unit still starts even if wanted unit fails. Preferred over Requires.

BindsTo.description: Stronger than Requires: this unit is stopped when the bound unit is stopped (not just when it fails to start).

PartOf.description: When the listed unit is stopped or restarted, this unit is also stopped/restarted. Does not affect starting.

Conflicts.description: Negative dependency. Starting this unit stops conflicting units and vice versa.

ConditionPathExists.description: Check if path exists before starting. Prefix with ! to negate. Prefix with - to make non-fatal.

ConditionFileNotEmpty.description: Check if file exists and is not empty.

ConditionDirectoryNotEmpty.description: Check if directory exists and is not empty.

AssertPathExists.description: Like ConditionPathExists but causes a failure (not skip) if not met.

OnFailure.description: Units to activate when this unit enters a 'failed' state. Useful for alerting.

OnSuccess.description: Units to activate when this unit succeeds (deactivates cleanly).

Type.description: Service startup type. simple: process is the service. forking: forks to background. notify: sends sd_notify(). oneshot: runs and exits. exec: like simple but 'started' after exec() succeeds.

ExecStart.description: Main command to start the service. Must be an absolute path. Use $MAINPID for the main process PID.

ExecStartPre.description: Commands to run before ExecStart. Prefix with '-' to ignore failure. Prefix with '+' to run as root.

ExecStartPost.description: Commands to run after ExecStart. Runs after the service is considered 'started'.

ExecStop.description: Command to stop the service. If not set, SIGTERM is sent. $MAINPID holds the main process PID.

ExecStopPost.description: Commands to run after the service is stopped. Runs even on failure.

Restart.description: When to restart: on-failure (non-zero exit, signal, timeout, watchdog). always: restart unconditionally. on-abnormal: signal, timeout, watchdog.

RestartSec.description: Time to wait before restarting. Default: 100ms. Accepts s, min, h, ms, us units.

StartLimitBurst.description: Maximum number of starts within StartLimitIntervalSec before the unit is held back. Default: 5.

StartLimitIntervalSec.description: Time window for start rate limiting. Default: 10s. Set to 0 to disable.

TimeoutStartSec.description: Timeout for service to start. If exceeded, service is killed (SIGTERM then SIGKILL). Default: 90s.

TimeoutStopSec.description: Timeout for service to stop. After this, SIGKILL is sent. Default: 90s.

WatchdogSec.description: Watchdog timeout. Service must call sd_notify(WATCHDOG=1) within this interval or be restarted.

KillMode.description: How to kill processes: control-group (all in cgroup), mixed (SIGTERM main, SIGKILL rest), process (main only).

KillSignal.description: Signal to send when stopping. Default: SIGTERM. Also: SIGINT, SIGQUIT, etc.

User.description: Run the service as this user. Creates a user namespace if DynamicUser=yes.

Group.description: Run the service with this group.

DynamicUser.description: Allocate a dynamic user/group at runtime. Automatically enables other sandboxing options.

WorkingDirectory.description: Set working directory before starting. Special: ~ for user home, - prefix to ignore if missing.

RootDirectory.description: Set root directory (chroot) for the service.

Environment.description: Set environment variables. For multiple: use multiple Environment= lines or EnvironmentFile=.

EnvironmentFile.description: Read environment variables from file (KEY=value format). Prefix with - to ignore if missing.

StandardOutput.description: Where to send stdout. journal: systemd journal. file:path: write to file. append:path: append.

StandardError.description: Where to send stderr. inherit: same as StandardOutput.

SyslogIdentifier.description: Identifier for syslog/journal entries. Defaults to process name. Used with journalctl -u.

PIDFile.description: Path to PID file. Required for Type=forking so systemd can track the main process.

RemainAfterExit.description: Service remains active after main process exits. Essential for Type=oneshot.

NotifyAccess.description: Which processes can send sd_notify() messages. Required for Type=notify.

LimitNOFILE.description: Maximum open file descriptors. Commonly set to 65535 for servers. Maps to ulimit -n.

LimitNPROC.description: Maximum number of processes for the user. Maps to ulimit -u.

LimitCORE.description: Core dump file size. Set to infinity for debugging, 0 for production.

LimitMEMLOCK.description: Maximum locked-in-memory size. Set to infinity for apps like Elasticsearch.

MemoryMax.description: Hard cgroup memory limit. Service is OOM-killed if exceeded. Supports K, M, G, T suffixes.

MemoryHigh.description: Soft cgroup memory limit. Processes throttled (swapped) but not killed.

CPUQuota.description: CPU time limit. 100% = 1 CPU core. 200% = 2 cores.

TasksMax.description: Maximum tasks (threads + processes). Default: 4915 (15% of kernel limit).

NoNewPrivileges.description: Prevent privilege escalation via setuid/setgid binaries. Always enable for untrusted services.

ProtectSystem.description: Filesystem protection. yes: /usr,/boot readonly. full: + /etc readonly. strict: everything readonly (use ReadWritePaths= for exceptions).

ProtectHome.description: Protect home directories. yes: inaccessible. tmpfs: empty tmpfs mounted over /home.

PrivateTmp.description: Private /tmp and /var/tmp. Prevents temp file race conditions between services.

PrivateDevices.description: Private /dev without physical devices. Only pseudo-devices like /dev/null, /dev/zero.

ProtectKernelTunables.description: Make /proc/sys, /sys readonly. Prevents kernel parameter modification.

ProtectKernelLogs.description: Deny access to kernel log ring buffer (/dev/kmsg).

ReadWritePaths.description: Whitelist writable paths when using ProtectSystem=strict.

CapabilityBoundingSet.description: Restrict Linux capabilities. Empty = no capabilities. Common: CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE (ports < 1024).

AmbientCapabilities.description: Capabilities given to unprivileged processes. Allows binding port 80 without root.

SystemCallArchitectures.description: Restrict syscall ABIs. 'native' = only the host architecture.

RestrictNamespaces.signature: RestrictNamespaces=yes|no|cgroup ipc net mnt pid user uts
RestrictNamespaces.description: Restrict namespace creation. yes = deny all. Or specify allowed types.

RestrictAddressFamilies.description: Restrict socket address families. Limits network access by protocol.

MemoryDenyWriteExecute.description: Deny memory mappings that are both writable and executable. Prevents JIT and code injection.

LockPersonality.description: Lock the execution personality (prevents changing ABI).

WantedBy.description: Target that pulls this unit via 'systemctl enable'. multi-user.target = boot. timers.target = timer units.

RequiredBy.description: Hard requirement from target. Rarely used — prefer WantedBy.

Also.description: Additional units to enable/disable together with this unit.

OnCalendar.description: Calendar event expression. Format: DayOfWeek Year-Month-Day Hour:Min:Sec. Shortcuts: minutely, hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, yearly.

OnBootSec.description: Trigger timer this long after boot.

OnUnitActiveSec.description: Trigger this long after the associated unit was last activated. Creates repeating timer.

OnUnitInactiveSec.description: Trigger this long after the associated unit was last deactivated.

Persistent.description: If timer missed while system was off, trigger immediately on next boot.

RandomizedDelaySec.description: Add random delay to prevent multiple timers firing simultaneously (thundering herd).

AccuracySec.description: Timer accuracy. Lower = more precise, higher = more battery-friendly. Default: 1min.

%i.description: Instance name (between @ and suffix). E.g., 8080 in myapp@8080.service.

$MAINPID.description: PID of the main process. Available in ExecReload=, ExecStop=.
